title: Privacy Advocates Push DPC to Conclude Vantara Probe
body: DataWatch EU has written directly to the Data Protection Commission urging it to complete its inquiry into Vantara AI before any acquisition or licensing arrangement with a third party changes the company's structure. In the letter, whose contents were shared with this publication, the group argues that the Hanseong licensing deal announced last week could alter how Vantara processes European data and potentially render parts of the investigation moot. A DPC spokesperson said the commission is "aware of recent developments" regarding Vantara and is monitoring the situation. Privacy law experts interviewed for this article disagreed about whether a licensing deal — as opposed to an acquisition — would legally affect the scope of the inquiry. One lawyer said the DPC's jurisdiction is over Vantara's processing activities, not its corporate arrangements; another said the data flows created by the Hanseong partnership may themselves require notification to the regulator. Vantara declined to confirm whether it had notified the DPC of the new agreement.
